name: Bolaso Reservoir and San Bartolomé
name_original: Estanque del Bolaso y Embalse de San Bartolomé — circular desde Ejea de los Caballeros
description: Enjoy an easy 17.3-mile touring cycling route around Estanque del Bolaso and Embalse de San Bartolomé from Ejea de los Caballeros.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ejea de los Caballeros offers diverse landscapes, from semi-desert terrain to forested hills and wetlands. The region is characterized by the unique rock formations of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, including the Bardena Aragonesa with its blackish pine forests and "rallas." Rivers such as the Arma de Luisa and Biel traverse the area, while the Estanca del Gancho provides a significant wetlands environment. These varied natural features create a range of conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Ejea de los Caballeros The most popular touring cycling route is Bolaso Reservoir and San Bartolomé, an easy 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete, passing by the Bolaso Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castildetierra Hill – Castildetierra loop from Pinsoro, a difficult 27.4 miles (44.2 km) path. This route explores the distinctive semi-desert landscape around the Castildetierra natural monument. Local touring cyclists also love the Luesia Village – Luesia Fountain loop from Ejea de los Caballeros, a 51.4 miles (82.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards Luesia Village, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Ejea de los Caballeros is defined by semi-desert landscapes, pine forests, and wetland areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Ejea de los Caballeros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ejea de los Caballeros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes will I encounter while touring cycling around Ejea de los Caballeros?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes, from the unique semi-desert terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring lunar-like formations and distinctive 'rallas' (rock walls), to forested hills and significant wetlands like the &lt;strong&gt;Estanca del Gancho&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also find areas characterized by blackish pine forests, especially in the Bardena Aragonesa.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Ejea de los Caballeros?
A: There are over a dozen touring cycling routes around Ejea de los Caballeros, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, leisurely rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994028730/bolaso-reservoir-and-san-bartolome" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaso Reservoir and San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 17.3 miles (27.8 km) long and takes approximately 1 hour 41 minutes to complete, passing by the scenic Bolaso Reservoir.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore several natural highlights. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt; natural monument, with its distinctive earthen castle shape, is a must-see. The vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers unique semi-desert scenery, and you might also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rallón Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31503161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Village – Luesia Fountain loop from Ejea de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51.4 miles (82.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain at Plaza de la Magdalena – Forest Rangers' House loop from Ejea de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt;, covering 58.8 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What historical and cultural sites can I visit in Ejea de los Caballeros?
A: Ejea de los Caballeros boasts a rich heritage. You can visit the &lt;strong&gt;Aquagraria Museum&lt;/strong&gt;, which houses an impressive collection of agricultural machinery, or explore the town's historical churches like Santa María (12th century) and El Salvador (13th century). The &lt;strong&gt;Casa del Colono&lt;/strong&gt; offers insights into the region's colonization history, and remnants of an ancient Jewish Quarter can also be found.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38282727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra Hill – Castildetierra loop from Pinsoro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.4 miles (44.2 km) path that explores the distinctive semi-desert landscape around the Castildetierra natural monument. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain at Plaza de la Magdalena – La Llana Viewpoint loop from Ejea de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 31.9 km ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Ejea de los Caballeros?
A: The routes in Ejea de los Caballeros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the unique semi-desert landscapes to the peaceful forested areas, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding experience.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ejea de los Caballeros?
A: The best times for touring cycling are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, especially in the semi-desert areas, while winters can be cooler. Checking the local weather forecast before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rallón Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provide stunning panoramic views of the unique landscapes, particularly within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on the touring cycling routes?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Easy routes like Bolaso Reservoir and San Bartolomé have minimal elevation changes (around 147 meters), while difficult routes such as the Luesia Village loop can involve substantial climbs, with elevation gains of over 950 meters.
tips: The El Bolaso reservoir is an ideal spot for fishing and is recognized as a fishing zone, an Aragon fishing license is required.
Nice views of the San Bartolomé Reservoir from the road. We can stop to rest and recover energy in the picnic area with tables under the shade of the pine trees next to the reservoir.

We are in the second largest municipality in Aragon, the City of Ejea de los Caballeros is located in the Cinco Villas region and offers us a diversity of places of interest such as churches located in the old town (Church of Santa María de la Corona , that of San Salvador or that of Nuestra Señora de la Oliva) or museums (The Aquagraria museum located in the City of Water and the Art and Exhibition Center) apart from beautiful nature such as the Estanca del Gancho or the walks of the Arba River in biel.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for touring bicycles and generally takes about 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete. This is an estimated duration for a steady pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Estanque del Bolaso and Embalse de San Bartolomé cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied but predominantly firm, making for a smooth ride. You'll encounter about 13.2 km of paved track and 12.9 km of asphalt. There's a smaller section of unpaved track, roughly 1.73 km, and the route also includes designated paths, regular roads, national roads, and some street sections.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is classified as easy and is suitable for touring cyclists of all fitness levels, including beginners and families. With only about 150 meters of positive elevation gain, it's very accessible.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy nice views of the San Bartolomé Reservoir from the road. The route also passes by the beautiful Estanca de El Bolaso. Additionally, the Church of Santa María de la Corona in Ejea de los Caballeros is a notable landmark near the start/end.

faqs.question: Are there any places to rest or have a picnic along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a pleasant picnic area with tables shaded by pine trees located next to the San Bartolomé Reservoir. It's a great spot to take a break and recharge.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the start of the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route conveniently starts near a car park in Ejea de los Caballeros, making access straightforward for those arriving by car.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the route can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable during those months.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially on shared paths and roads. Consider your dog's comfort with cycling alongside a bike for this duration.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that starts and ends in Ejea de los Caballeros, making it convenient for returning to your starting point.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de la Corona, Ejea de los Caballeros
